original: Byzantine General Belisarius makes his formal entry into Carthage, having conquered it from the Vandals
Khanate of Kazan is conquered by troops of Ivan Grozny
Spanish general strategist Bernardino de Mendoza occupies fort Rhine
Paul de Chomedy de Maisonneuve claims Montreal
Prince William of Orangeappointed viceroy of Overijssel
Jews of Lublin are massacred
Asser Levy granted butcher's license (kosher meat) in New Amsterdam
Torsåker witch trials begin, largest witch trials in Sweden, 71 beheaded and burned
English fleet under Lord Peterborough occupies Barcelona
Cornelis Steenoven is 1st archbishop of Old Catholic church in Utrecht, The Netherlands
Saxon army surrenders to Prussia
Mjr James Graves Simcoe appointed commandant of Queen's Rangers (Penn)
Jean Pilstre de Rozier makes 1st tethered balloon ascent
Earliest 32°F (0°C) recorded temperature in NYC
American Dr William Thomas Green Morton's 1st public use of ether
Cliff House opens in San Francisco, 1st of many on the site
Confederate troops occupy Glasgow, Missouri
Great fire in Quebec destroys 2,500 houses
First session of the US 45th Congress (1877-79) convenes in Washington D.C.
Edison Electric Light Company incorporated
Mexican soldiers kill Victorio, one of the greatest Apache military strategists
1st American fishing magazine, "American Angler" published
US Supreme Court declares Civil Rights Act of 1875 unconstitutional
Charles "Old Hoss" Radbourn pitches his 60th win of season for MLB Providence Grays in 8-0 win over Quakers at Philadelphia
Amsterdam Central Station officially opens
Alabama Penny Savings Bank organizes in Birmingham
Henry Perky patents a machine he developed with William Ford for the preparation of cereals for food, otherwise known as shredded wheat
Aaron and Samuel Bloch carry 1st Mail Pouch

MLB Cleveland Spiders close out the season losing both ends of a doubleheader, 16-1 & 19-3 to the Cincinnati Reds on the road; Spiders end year with record of 20-134, still the standard for fewest wins
The Russians are driven back by the Japanese in the Battle of Shaho; both sides suffer high casualties: Japanese (16,000) and Russians (60,000)
Union workers at NVV rejects safety demands
Train crash at St. James' Station, Liverpool during "Black Week" kills six and injures 63
Battle of Warsaw, begins (ends Oct 21)
US Clayton Anti-trust Act passed (union & strike rights)
British Q-ship Cymric sinks British submarine J6
14 horses begin 300-mile race from Vermont to Massachusetts for $1000 prize money

American pianist Henry Cowell sparks a riot among audience members at Gewandhaus concert hall in Leipzig, Germany - some tried to physically stop his avant-garde performance, while others defended him
Willem Landré's opera "Beatrice" premieres in The Hague
Austria government of Seipel, forms
Philip Barry's play "White Wings!" premieres in NYC
Tata Airlines (later to become Air India) makes its first flight
20th Amendment to the US Constitution goes into effect, changing the start of a Presidential term from March to January
Philadelphia Eagles play 1st NFL game, lose to NY Giants 56-0
NHL's St Louis Eagles fold
LaGuardia Airport opens in NYC
Yeshiva of Mir closes after 124 years
-16] Heavy German air raid on London, 400 killed
London's Waterloo Station bombed by German luftwaffe
First mass deportation of German, Austrian, and Czech Jews to Eastern Europe for "resettlement" in labor camps
Jews caught outside Nazi Ghetto walls in occupied Poland could be put to death

WWII: Nazi SS-Brigadier General Walter Stahlecker submits  summary report documenting killing of more than 118,000 unarmed Jewish men, women, and children by men under his command between June 22 and October 15, 1941 during invasion of the Soviet Union to the Reich Security Main Office in Berlin,
German 6th Army occupies Tractorenfabriek, 3,000 Germans die
The Arrow Cross Party (very similar to Hitler's NSDAP (Nazi party)) takes power in Hungary
Baseball Attendance hits record 10.28 million (Tigers 1.28 is highest)
China's Red army occupies Chinchou in Guangdong
Administration of territory of Manipur taken over by Indian government
Tripura accedes to Indian union
Arthur Laurent's "Time of the Cuckoo" premieres in NYC
John Patrick's play "Teahouse of the August Moon" premieres in New York
KOIN TV channel 6 in Portland, OR (CBS) begins broadcasting
WJNL (now WFAT) TV channel 19 in Johnstown, PA (IND) begins
Hurricane Hazel makes landfall in the US in North Carolina as a category 4 hurricane, 195 die in US and Canada
KLTV TV channel 7 in Tyler-Longview, TX (ABC) begins broadcasting
Country music radio show "Grand Ole Opry" is first broadcast on television in a live hour-long show by ABC
First plane to land safely on water - Pan Am Flight 6 San Francisco to Honolulu, all 24 passengers and 7 crew survive
Pyotr Bolotnikov runs world record 10k (28:42.8)
William J. Brennan, Jr. is appointed to US Supreme Court
Giants trade Minneapolis franchise to Red Sox for SF Seals-franchises only, not the players
Tunisia drops diplomatic relations with Egypt
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya, USSR
KNDO TV channel 23 in Yakima, WA (NBC) begins broadcasting
Byron R. White appointed to the US Supreme Court
WLOX TV channel 13 in Biloxi-Gulfport, MS (ABC) begins broadcasting
American Don Schollander swims a world record 4:12.2 to win the 400m freestyle gold medal at the Tokyo Olympics; second of Schollander's 4 gold medals at the Games
American sprinter Bob Hayes equals world record 10.0s to beat Cuba's Enrique Figuerola by 0.2s and win the men's 100m gold medal at the Tokyo Olympics
Australian swimmer Ian O'Brien sets world record 2:27.8 to beat Soviet Georgy Prokopenko by 0.4s and win men's 200m breaststroke gold medal at the Tokyo Olympics
Soviet rower Vyacheslav Ivanov wins his third consecutive Olympic men's single sculls gold medal at the Tokyo Games; also the reigning World and European champion

US women's 4 x 100m freestyle relay team of Sharon Stouder, Donna de Varona, Lillian Watson & Kathy Ellis swims world record 4:03.8 to beat Australia by 3.1s and win the gold medal at the Tokyo Olympics
WEMT (now WVII) TV channel 7 in Bangor, ME (ABC) begins broadcasting
Australia bans Troggs' "I Can't Control Myself" as "terribly obscene"
Al Oerterof the US wins the men's discus in an Olympic record 64.78m at the Mexico City Games; Oerter's unprecedented 4th consecutive Olympic discus title
American sprinter Wyomia Tyus wins her second consecutive Olympic 100m title with a world record 11.08s; beats teammate Barbara Ferrell by 0.08s for the gold medal at the Mexico City Games
Australian Ralph Doubell runs a world record 1:44.40 to beat Kenyan Wilson Kiprugut and win the men's 800m at the Mexico City Olympics
British athlete David Hemery sets a world record 48.12s to beat Gerhard Hennige of West Germany and take the gold medal in the men's 400m hurdles at the Mexico City Olympics
MLB American League expansion draft: Kansas City Royals pick Baltimore Orioles pitcher Roger Nelson & Seattle Pilots pick California Angels infielder Don Mincher 1st as they choose 30 players each
The Nationalist Party of Northern Ireland (NPNI) withdraws from its role as 'official' opposition within the Northern Ireland parliament at Stormont
Bank of America World Headquarters (555 California) dedicated
Madison Square Garden TV Network begins (Rangers vs North Stars)
Vietnam Moratorium Day in the US, millions nationwide protest against the war
Bridge over Yarra River in Melbourne crashes; killing 35
Russian passenger flight hijacked to Turkey
The start of the 2,500-year celebration of Iran, celebrating the birth of Persia
World's first arcade video game Computer Space developed by Syzygy first demonstrated at the MOA Show in the US
Omni in Atlanta opens - Hawks beat NY Knicks 109-101
Tanks attack Thailand demonstrating students, 300 killed
Massachusetts Governor Francis W. Sargent mobilizes National Guard troops to restore order in Boston after 'school busing' desegregation program spurs racial violence
NHL Washington Capitals earn 1st franchise tie, playing LA Kings to 1-1 draw
Nobel Prize for Chemistry awarded to Paul J. Flory for work on macro molecules
Iceland moves intl boundary from 50 to 200 miles
"Serpentine Fire" single released by Earth, Wind & Fire (Billboard Song of the Year 1978)
Arkansas' Steve Little kicks a record tying 67 yard field goal
Debbie Boone's single "You Light Up My Life" goes #1 & stays #1 for 10 weeks
Don Ritchie runs world record 100 mile (11:30:51)
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
1st Monday night game from NYC, Jets beat Vikings 14-7 (Shea Stad)
Chinese premier Hwa Kwofeng visits Paris
Military coup in El Salvador: President Carlos Romero flees
Nobel Prize in Economics awarded to Lawrence Klein
Professional cheerleader Krazy George Henderson leads what is thought to be the first audience wave in Oakland, California.
Columbia beats Yale 21-18 in football, will lose next 44 games
NHL Chicago Black Hawks & Toronto Maple Leafs combine for fastest 5 goals (84 seconds), in a 10-8 Leaf win in Toronto
US Marine sharpshooters kill 5 snipers at Beirut International Airport
Central Intelligence Agency Information Act passes
Dutch Centrum Party expels 2nd Member of parliament Hans Janmaat due to fraud
Nobel prize for economics awarded to Franco Modigliani
Shelley Taylor of Australia makes fastest swim ever around Manhattan Island, doing it in 6 hours 12 minutes 29 seconds
Shuttle Columbia carries Spacelab into orbit
Longest post season game, Mets beat Astros 7-6 in 16 & win NL pennant
6th Belgium government of Martens falls
Coup in Burkina Faso, president Sankara dies
Lanford Wilson's play "Burn This" premieres in NYC
NFL Players Association orders an end to 24 day strike; union fails to achieve demands short-term with no collective bargaining agreement in place
The Great Storm of 1987 hits France and England
NCAA record rushing yardage (768 yards-Oklahoma)

Oh-My-God particle - most powerful cosmic ray ever detected, by Fly's Eye camera at Dugway Proving Ground, Utah, measuring 320 EeV - 40million x more powerful than any terrestrial particle accelerator
Howard Stern Radio Show begins broadcasting on WLUP-AM, Chicago, Illinois
NYC Subway motorman Robert Ray convicted of manslaughter in death of 5 riders, when he fell asleep drunk while in control of train
Amstel brewery on Curacao produces 1,000,000,000th bottle
Late night talk show "The Chevy Chase Show" final episode airs on FOX tv; cancelled after 6 weeks
Botswana President Ketumile Masires BDP wins parliamentary election
Philip Glass' Symphony No. 2, for string orchestra, premieres with the Brooklyn Philharmonic Orchestra conducted by Dennis Russell Davies, at the Brooklyn Academy of Music in Brooklyn, NYC
President Jean-Baptiste Aristide returns to Haiti
Carolina Panthers win 1st game ever beating NY Jets 26-15
Britain's Andy Green sets jet-powered car record (763.035 mph)
Former US Representative Dan Rostenkowski released from custody for mail fraud
Nobel Prize for Chemistry awarded to Jens Christain Skou, Paul D. Boyer and John E. Walker for their work on enzymes in the body
US launches nuclear powered Cassini to Saturn
ICC Men's Cricket KnockOut Trophy, Gymkhana Club, Nairobi: New Zealand win their first international title with a 4 wicket win over India; Man of the Match: Chris Cairns, NZ 102no (113)
NASA's Galileo spacecraft passes within 112 miles of Jupiter's moon Io.
The first tanker loading of the new $2.5-billion Kazakh-Russia Pipeline takes place
KingHenry VIIIof England orders bowling lanes at Whitehall
King of SpainCharles VnamesHernán Cortésgovernor of Mexico
Commissioned byCatherine de' Medici, the first ballet "Ballet Comique de la Reine" is staged in Paris
Future King of EnglandCharles IIflees England and pursuing Parliamentary forces, sailing from Shoreham, West Sussex to France
Edward Gibbonobserves a group of friars singing in the ruined Temple of Jupiter in Rome, which inspires him to begin work on The History of the Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire
1st US presidential tour byGeorge Washingtonin New England

CaptainAlfred Dreyfusarrested and accused of espionage in France
KingLeopold IItakes Belgian crown

Rather than accept any trade offers, the Yanks releaseTony Lazzeri
Hideki Tojoappointed Prime Minister of Imperial Japan

First synthesis of an oral contraceptive (norethindrone) made by Luis E. Miramontes under direction ofCarl Djerassiand George Rosenkranz at Mexican drug company Syntex
"Charlotte's Web" byE. B. Whiteand illustrated by Garth Williams is published by Harper & Brothers

NY Knicks retire 2nd number, # 10,Walt Frazier

Gregorian calendar is introduced in Spain, Portugal and the Papal States, after skipping 10 days from October 4 to sync the calendar to the solar year and compensate for the drift that had occurred due to the Julian calendar having too many leap days
